campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to hear a financial report from the regional transit authority covering March 2026 investments, discuss a new policy limiting use of city property for federal civil enforcement activities, and address staffing challenges including job openings and employee retention.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority financial report for March 2026
New city policy restricting city property use for federal civil enforcement
City job vacancies and efforts to recruit and keep employees

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing about cleaning up hazardous conditions on certain properties that the County identified. The council also approved a new technology policy and established a process for residents to request special accommodations from the city.
keyTopics: Public hearing on hazardous property cleanup and community objections
County-identified properties requiring abatement work
New Technology Disruption Policy for the city
Process for requesting reasonable accommodations from the city

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — May 19, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on May 19, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, review finances from March, and authorize several spending and contracting decisions. The council approved a road paving contract, accepted a donated defibrillator (AED), and moved forward with grant applications and a multi-year service agreement.
keyTopics: Approved O'Grady Paving construction contract for road work
Accepted donation of Automated External Defibrillator (AED)
Authorized five-year service agreement
Submitted grant applications for statewide funding
Set project list and budget priorities for fiscal year 2026-2027

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 20, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on May 20, 2026 to plan upcoming agendas and review committee work plans. The council cancelled their May 26 meeting and discussed what items to add to their June 2 draft agenda, including updates to neighborhood services and government rules committees.
keyTopics: May 26 meeting cancelled
June 2 agenda items under review
Neighborhood Services and Education Committee work plan update
Rules and Open Government Committee work plan review
Event approvals sponsored by council members

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 20, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met on May 20, 2026 to approve meeting minutes, hear public comments, and select members for the Youth Advisory Committee for the upcoming fiscal year. The council also reviewed upcoming agendas for various city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Youth Advisory Committee member selection for 2026-27
Public comment period
Approval of previous meeting minutes
Review of upcoming board and commission agendas

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 19, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a regular meeting on May 19, 2026, to conduct city business and recognize two important observances. The council acknowledged Asian Pacific American Heritage Month and Affordable Housing Month during the meeting.
keyTopics: Recognition of Asian Pacific American Heritage Month
Recognition of Affordable Housing Month
Approval of April meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 19, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to update the city's long-term health and safety plans for 2015-2040 and recognized the Public Works Department for their work on roads, parks, utilities, and sustainability. The council also celebrated Asian American Pacific Islander Month.
keyTopics: Health and Safety Element update to the General Plan (2015-2040)
Public Works Department recognition during Public Works Week
Asian American Pacific Islander (AAPI) Month celebration

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara Station Area Task Force held a special meeting on May 21, 2026, at City Hall to discuss planning and development issues related to the station area. The meeting was open to the public both in person and via Zoom.
keyTopics: Station area planning and development
Public meeting notice compliance with California law
City Hall accessibility (East Wing location)

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 19, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026, where they voted on several land development and zoning matters, passed an emergency ordinance to add new city rules, and appointed a permanent city manager. Residents had opportunities to speak during public hearings on these items.
keyTopics: Emergency ordinance passed adding new city regulations
New zoning rules introduced for public review
Site development permit approved for a development project
Permanent city manager appointed

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 15, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council heard an update on Palo Alto's plan to help residents switch from gas to electric heating and appliances. The council discussed proposed principles for electrification programs designed to help the city comply with air quality regulations from the Bay Area Air Quality Management District.
keyTopics: Residential electrification status update - progress on helping homes convert from gas to electric
Proposed principles for electrification assistance programs
Air District regulation compliance requirements
Staff presentation on implementation approach
